items.title: Phase stability regulator based on two dynamic parameters for autonomous mobile robots
items.summary: Autonomous mobile robots, or AMRs, can benefit from a phase regulator with two real-time signals, according to a researcher. The post Phase stability regulator based on two dynamic parameters for autonomous mobile robots appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: FAULHABER designs DualGear for autonomous logistics systems
items.summary: FAULHABER has designed DualGear to offer high performance in space-constrained autonomous logistics applications. The post FAULHABER designs DualGear for autonomous logistics systems appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: The art of crossing the chasm: When is a startup ready for enterprise adoption?
items.summary: Silicon Foundry bridges the chasm between startups and enterprises, focusing on innovation, strategic partnerships, and navigating macroeconomic shifts. The post The art of crossing the chasm: When is a startup ready for enterprise adoption? appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: Top 10 robotics stories of April 2026
items.summary: In April 2026, prominent robotics companies hit technical milestones, raised large funding rounds, and took part in patent disputes. The post Top 10 robotics stories of April 2026 appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: Why deformable materials are physical AI’s real manufacturing test
items.summary: Physical AI can tackle assembly of deformable materials in apparel and beyond -- with the right approach and software, says Createme's CEO. The post Why deformable materials are physical AI’s real manufacturing test appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: California lawmakers open the gate for autonomous trucks in the state
items.summary: The California DMV has paved the way for autonomous trucks in the state with expanded safety and oversight requirements for all autonomous vehicles. The post California lawmakers open the gate for autonomous trucks in the state appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: Teradyne Robotics revenue rises at the start of 2026
items.summary: Teradyne Robotics brought in $91 million in Q1 2026, with its AI products helping to boost robotics sales. The post Teradyne Robotics revenue rises at the start of 2026 appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: HERMES++: Toward a Unified Driving World Model for 3D Scene Understanding and Generation
items.summary: Driving world models serve as a pivotal technology for autonomous driving by simulating environmental dynamics. However, existing approaches predominantly focus on future scene generation, often overlooking comprehensive 3D scene understanding.

items.title: OmniRobotHome: A Multi-Camera Platform for Real-Time Multiadic Human-Robot Interaction
items.summary: Human-robot collaboration has been studied primarily in dyadic or sequential settings. However, real homes require multiadic collaboration, where multiple humans and robots share a workspace, acting concurrently on interleaved subtasks with tight spatial and temporal coupling.

items.title: LaST-R1: Reinforcing Action via Adaptive Physical Latent Reasoning for VLA Models
items.summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have increasingly incorporated reasoning mechanisms for complex robotic manipulation.

items.title: RopeDreamer: A Kinematic Recurrent State Space Model for Dynamics of Flexible Deformable Linear Objects
items.summary: The robotic manipulation of Deformable Linear Objects (DLOs) is a fundamental challenge due to the high-dimensional, non-linear dynamics of flexible structures and the complexity of maintaining topological integrity during contact-rich tasks.

items.title: FlexiTac: A Low-Cost, Open-Source, Scalable Tactile Sensing Solution for Robotic Systems
items.summary: We present FlexiTac, a low-cost, open-source, and scalable piezoresistive tactile sensing solution designed for robotic end-effectors. FlexiTac is a practical "plug-in" module consisting of (i) thin, flexible tactile sensor pads that provide dense tactile signals and (ii) a compact multi-channel readout board that streams synchronized measurements for real-time control and large-scale data collection.

items.title: Design and Characteristics of a Thin-Film ThermoMesh for the Efficient Embedded Sensing of a Spatio-Temporally Sparse Heat Source
items.summary: This work presents ThermoMesh, a passive thin-film thermoelectric mesh sensor designed to detect and characterize spatio-temporally sparse heat sources through conduction-based thermal imaging. The device integrates thermoelectric junctions with linear or nonlinear interlayer resistive elements to perform simultaneous sensing and in-sensor compression.

items.title: FreeOcc: Training-Free Embodied Open-Vocabulary Occupancy Prediction
items.summary: Existing learning-based occupancy prediction methods rely on large-scale 3D annotations and generalize poorly across environments. We present FreeOcc, a training-free framework for open-vocabulary occupancy prediction from monocular or RGB-D sequences.

items.title: GSDrive: Reinforcing Driving Policies by Multi-mode Trajectory Probing with 3D Gaussian Splatting Environment
items.summary: End-to-end (E2E) autonomous driving presents a promising approach for translating perceptual inputs directly into driving actions. However, prohibitive annotation costs and temporal data quality degradation hinder long-term real-world deployment.

items.title: Towards Neuro-symbolic Causal Rule Synthesis, Verification, and Evaluation Grounded in Legal and Safety Principles
items.summary: Rule-based systems remain central in safety-critical domains but often struggle with scalability, brittleness, and goal misspecification. These limitations can lead to reward hacking and failures in formal verification, as AI systems tend to optimize for narrow objectives.

items.title: Framework for Collaborative Operation of Autonomous Delivery Vehicles Within a Marshaling Yard
items.summary: As autonomous vehicles slowly deploy into urban roads for limited use cases with significant edge case issues, closed facilities like marshaling yards provide a ripe case for combining lower-level vehicle autonomy with fixed infrastructure to create full autonomy without similar edge case concerns.

items.title: Dreaming Across Towns: Semantic Rollout and Town-Adversarial Regularization for Zero-Shot Held-Out-Town Fixed-Route Driving in CARLA
items.summary: Learned driving agents often degrade when deployed in unseen environments. This paper studies a deliberately bounded instance of that problem in the CARLA simulator: zero-shot transfer of a closed-loop fixed-route driving agent from Town05 and Town06 to unseen Town03 and Town04. The study isolates structural town shift by keeping weather fixed to ClearNoon and removing traffic and pedestrians.

items.title: Flying by Inference: Active Inference World Models for Adaptive UAV Swarms
items.summary: This paper presents an expert-guided active-inference-inspired framework for adaptive UAV swarm trajectory planning. The proposed method converts multi-UAV trajectory design from a repeated combinatorial optimization problem into a hierarchical probabilistic inference problem.

items.title: Synthetic Biological Intelligence: System-Level Abstractions and Adaptive Bio-Digital Interaction
items.summary: Concurrent advances across fields such as organoid technology, Microelectrode Arrays (MEAs), neuromorphic computing, and machine learning have given rise to a groundbreaking research paradigm: Synthetic Biological Intelligence (SBI).

items.title: Launchpad Build AI offers MLM to speed industrial automation design
items.summary: Launchpad Build AI says its Manufacturing Language Model can democratize automation for high-mix, low-volume production with inputs from photos, videos, or CAD. The post Launchpad Build AI offers MLM to speed industrial automation design appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: Learning-Based Hierarchical Scene Graph Matching for Robot Localization Leveraging Prior Maps
items.summary: Accurate localization is a fundamental requirement for autonomous robots operating in indoor environments. Scene graphs encode the spatial structure of an environment as a hierarchy of semantic entities and their relationships, and can be constructed both online from robot sensor data and offline from architectural priors such as Building Information Models (BIM).

items.title: MotuBrain: An Advanced World Action Model for Robot Control
items.summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models achieve strong semantic generalization but often lack fine-grained modeling of world dynamics. Recent work explores video generation models as a foundation for world modeling, leading to unified World Action Models (WAMs) that jointly model visual dynamics and actions.

items.title: Connected Dependability Cage: Run-Time Function and Anomaly Monitoring for the Development and Operation of Safe Automated Vehicles
items.summary: The advancement of automated vehicles introduces complex safety challenges, particularly in dynamic and unpredictable environments where AI-enabled perception systems must operate reliably. Ensuring compliance with safety standards such as ISO 26262 and ISO/PAS 21448 (SOTIF) is essential for addressing system malfunctions and mitigating unsafe behavior in unknown scenarios.

items.title: ExoActor: Exocentric Video Generation as Generalizable Interactive Humanoid Control
items.summary: Humanoid control systems have made significant progress in recent years, yet modeling fluent interaction-rich behavior between a robot, its surrounding environment, and task-relevant objects remains a fundamental challenge. This difficulty arises from the need to jointly capture spatial context, temporal dynamics, robot actions, and task intent at scale, which is a poor match to conventional supervision.

items.title: Robust Geometric Control of Catenary Robots under Unstructured Force Uncertainties
items.summary: This paper considers the robust control of a catenary robot composed of two quadrotors connected by an inextensible cable. The system is modeled on \(SE(3)\), with the cable treated as a geometric subsystem induced by the UAV configuration rather than as an independent dynamical element. The catenary shape determines configuration-dependent forces that couple the translational dynamics of the vehicles.

items.title: Can Tabular Foundation Models Guide Exploration in Robot Policy Learning?
items.summary: Policy optimization in high-dimensional continuous control for robotics remains a challenging problem. Predominant methods are inherently local and often require extensive tuning and carefully chosen initial guesses for good performance, whereas more global and less initialization-sensitive search methods typically incur high rollout costs.

items.title: Robot Learning from Human Videos: A Survey
items.summary: A critical bottleneck hindering further advancement in embodied AI and robotics is the challenge of scaling robot data. To address this, the field of learning robot manipulation skills from human video data has attracted rapidly growing attention in recent years, driven by the abundance of human activity videos and advances in computer vision.

items.title: Simulating Infant First-Person Sensorimotor Experience via Motion Retargeting from Babies to Humanoids
items.summary: Motion retargeting from humans to human-like artificial agents is becoming increasingly important as humanoid robots grow more capable. However, most existing approaches focus only on reproducing kinematics and ignore the rich sensorimotor experience associated with human movement.

items.title: World2Minecraft: Occupancy-Driven Simulated Scenes Construction
items.summary: Embodied intelligence requires high-fidelity simulation environments to support perception and decision-making, yet existing platforms often suffer from data contamination and limited flexibility. To mitigate this, we propose World2Minecraft to convert real-world scenes into structured Minecraft environments based on 3D semantic occupancy prediction.

items.title: XELA Robotics adds enhancements to uSkin sensor family ahead of Robotics Summit
items.summary: XELA Robotics will be showing its latest sensor technology at the Robotics Summit & Expo in Boston in May 2026. The post XELA Robotics adds enhancements to uSkin sensor family ahead of Robotics Summit appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: Schaeffler plans to deploy 1,000 Hexagon humanoids by 2032
items.summary: Schaeffler is deepening its investment in humanoid robots in production through partnerships with Hexagon and VinDynamics. The post Schaeffler plans to deploy 1,000 Hexagon humanoids by 2032 appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: Project CETI deploys autonomous underwater gliders to better observe sperm whales
items.summary: Project CETI has deployed a new underwater glider that can monitor whales for longer than the organization's previous methods. The post Project CETI deploys autonomous underwater gliders to better observe sperm whales appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: GFT Technologies AI-powered robots move from inspection to action for automakers
items.summary: GFT Technologies has built on its work with Google Cloud to deploy robots on automotive factory floors to find and remove defective parts. The post GFT Technologies AI-powered robots move from inspection to action for automakers appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: Flex and Teradyne expand partnership to scale physical AI
items.summary: Flex and Teradyne Robotics expanded their partnership to accelerate physical AI across global manufacturing facilities. The post Flex and Teradyne expand partnership to scale physical AI appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: Apptronik’s new CPO hire a major step in right direction
items.summary: Apptronik taps Waymo veteran Daniel Chu as CPO to lead its $935M transition from humanoid R&D to mass-market commercialization. The post Apptronik’s new CPO hire a major step in right direction appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: Appetronix acquires Cibotica to automate restaurant kitchens
items.summary: Until now, Appetronix has focused on creating standalone fully autonomous restaurants. With Cibotica, it can also retrofit existing kitchens. The post Appetronix acquires Cibotica to automate restaurant kitchens appeared first on The Robot Report .

items.title: Into the Omniverse: Manufacturing’s Simulation-First Era Has Arrived
items.summary: Manufacturing’s traditional design-build-test cycle rested on a single assumption: Real-world testing was the only reliable test environment.

items.title: On Distributed Control of Continuum Swarms: Local Controllers as Differential Operators
items.summary: We study the problem of distributed control of large-scale robotic swarms which can be modeled as continuum densities evolving under the continuity equation. We propose a formalization of distributed controllers as (generally nonlinear) differential operators, in which control inputs depend only on local information about the state and environment.

items.title: GazeVLA: Learning Human Intention for Robotic Manipulation
items.summary: Embodied foundation models have achieved significant breakthroughs in robotic manipulation, yet they still depend heavily on large-scale robot demonstrations. Although recent works have explored leveraging human data to alleviate this dependency, effectively extracting transferable knowledge remains a significant challenge due to the inherent embodiment gap between human and robot.

items.title: Clinical Evaluation of a Tongue-Controlled Wrist Abduction-Adduction Assistance in a 6-DoF Upper-Limb Exoskeleton for Individuals with ALS and SCI
items.summary: Upper-limb exoskeletons (ULEs) have the potential to restore functional independence in individuals with severe motor impairments; however, the clinical relevance of wrist degrees of freedom (DoF), particularly abduction-adduction (Ab-Ad), remains insufficiently evaluated. This study investigates the functional and user-perceived impact of wrist Ab-Ad assistance during two activities of daily living (ADLs).

items.title: Design, Modelling and Experimental Evaluation of a Tendon-driven Wrist Abduction-Adduction Mechanism for an upper limb exoskeleton
items.summary: Wrist exoskeletons play a vital role in rehabilitation and assistive applications, yet conventional actuation mechanisms such as electric motors or pneumatics often introduce undesirable weight, friction, and complexity. This paper presents a novel single-cable (tendon), torsional-spring-assisted actuation mechanism for wrist abduction-adduction, and a simulation-based method for selecting its stiffness parameters.

items.title: NVIDIA and Partners Showcase the Future of AI-Driven Manufacturing at Hannover Messe 2026
items.summary: Manufacturing is at an inflection point. Across every major industrial economy, the pressure to do more with less — due to faster design cycles, leaner operations and strain on skilled labor pools — is accelerating the shift to AI-driven production. The question is no longer whether to adopt AI, but how fast and at what […]

items.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
items.summary: This National Robotics Week, NVIDIA is highlighting the breakthroughs that are bringing AI into the physical world — as well as the growing wave of robots transforming industries, from agricultural and manufacturing to energy and beyond. Advancements in robot learning, simulation and foundation models are accelerating development, enabling robots to move from training in virtual […]

items.title: Into the Omniverse: NVIDIA GTC Showcases Virtual Worlds Powering the Physical AI Era
items.summary: Editor’s note: This post is part of Into the Omniverse, a series focused on how developers, 3D practitioners, and enterprises can transform their workflows using the latest advances in OpenUSD and NVIDIA Omniverse. NVIDIA GTC last week showcased a turning point in physical AI: Robots, vehicles and factories are scaling from single use cases and […]

items.title: NVIDIA GTC 2026: Live Updates on What’s Next in AI
items.summary: Rolling coverage from San Jose, including NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang’s keynote, news highlights, live demos and on‑the‑ground color through March 19.

items.title: From Simulation to Production: How to Build Robots With AI
items.summary: The latest open models and frameworks from NVIDIA bring together simulation, robot learning and embedded compute to accelerate cloud-to-robot workflows.

items.title: Gecko Robotics lands the largest US Navy robotics deal yet
items.summary: Gecko Robotics inked a five-year deal to help the U.S. Navy monitor and predict needed maintenance on its fleet of ships.

items.title: As Open Models Spark AI Boom, NVIDIA Jetson Brings It to Life at the Edge
items.summary: The Cat 306 CR mini-excavator weighs just under eight tons and fits inside a standard shipping container. It’s the machine a contractor rents when the job site is tight: a utility trench near a foundation, a basement dig in a dense neighborhood. The cab is roughly the size of a phone booth. The operator sits […]

items.title: Qualcomm’s partnership with Neura Robotics is just the beginning
items.summary: Neura Robotics is going to build new robots on top of Qualcomm's new IQ10 processors that were released at CES.

items.title: ABB Robotics Taps NVIDIA Omniverse to Deliver Industrial‑Grade Physical AI at Scale
items.summary: ABB Robotics and NVIDIA today announced a breakthrough partnership that brings industrial‑grade physical AI to the factory floor. By integrating NVIDIA Omniverse libraries directly into its RobotStudio programming and simulation suite, ABB Robotics will now deliver physically accurate simulation capabilities in its platform, dramatically cutting engineering time, reducing deployment costs by up to 40% and […]

items.title: NVIDIA and Global Industrial Software Leaders Partner With India’s Largest Manufacturers to Drive AI Boom
items.summary: India is entering a new age of industrialization, as AI transforms how the world designs, builds and runs physical products and systems. The country is investing $134 billion dollars in new manufacturing capacity across construction, automotive, renewable energy and robotics, creating both a massive challenge and opportunity to build software-defined factories from day one. At […]

items.title: Upside Robotics is reducing fertilizer use and waste in corn crops
items.summary: Upside Robotics builds autonomous solar-powered robots that can help farmers reduce their fertilizer use by 70%.

items.title: Into the Omniverse: Physical AI Open Models and Frameworks Advance Robots and Autonomous Systems
items.summary: Open source has become essential for driving innovation in robotics and autonomy. By providing access to critical infrastructure — from simulation frameworks to AI models — NVIDIA is enabling collaborative development that accelerates the path to safer, more capable autonomous systems.

items.title: TouchGuide: Inference-Time Steering of Visuomotor Policies via Touch Guidance
items.summary: Fine-grained and contact-rich manipulation remain challenging for robots, largely due to the underutilization of tactile feedback. To address this, we introduce TouchGuide, a novel cross-policy visuo-tactile fusion paradigm that fuses modalities within a low-dimensional action space. Specifically, TouchGuide operates in two stages to guide a pre-trained diffusion or flow-matching visuomotor policy at inference time.

items.title: How YC-backed Bucket Robotics survived its first CES
items.summary: Now, the startup is turning its attention to building the business, fundraising, and striking commercial deals.

items.title: Oshen built the first ocean robot to collect data in a Category 5 hurricane
items.summary: Oshen has signed contracts with multiple government agencies for its C-Star robots to collect ocean data autonomously.

items.title: AI’s Next Revolution: Multiply Labs Is Scaling Robotics-Driven Cell Therapy Biomanufacturing Labs
items.summary: Startup works with leading cell therapy companies to bring robotics manufacturing into the clean room, reducing costs by more than 70% while accelerating output compared with legacy systems.

items.title: Inside CES 2026’s “physical AI” takeover
items.summary: After years of chatbots and image generators, AI is finally leaving the screen. At CES 2026, that shift became impossible to ignore.  The annual tech showcase in Las Vegas was dominated by “physical AI” and robotics, from Boston Dynamic’s newly redesigned Atlas humanoid robot to AI-powered ice makers (yes, really). The companies in attendance clearly want consumers […]

items.title: Japan Science and Technology Agency Develops NVIDIA-Powered Moonshot Robot for Elderly Care
items.summary: The next universal technology since the smartphone is on the horizon — and it may be a little less pocket friendly. The Moonshot research program, funded by the Japan Science and Technology Agency and accelerated by NVIDIA AI and robotics technologies, is working to create a world by 2050 where AI-powered, autonomously learning robots are […]

items.title: Steel, Sensors and Silicon: How Caterpillar Is Bringing Edge AI to the Jobsite
items.summary: At CES, Caterpillar reveals how it’s integrating NVIDIA technologies, from NVIDIA Jetson Thor to speech models, to transform the world’s heavy industries.

items.title: From Warehouse to Wallet: New State of AI in Retail and CPG Survey Uncovers How AI Is Rewiring Supply Chains and Customer Experiences
items.summary: AI has transformed retail and consumer packaged goods (CPG) operations, enhancing customer analysis and segmentation to enable greater personalization for marketing and advertising, and boosting the speed and accuracy of demand forecasting for supply chains and logistics. Companies are also raising the bar for customer engagement through intelligent digital shopping assistants and catalog enrichment by […]

items.title: NVIDIA Unveils New Open Models, Data and Tools to Advance AI Across Every Industry
items.summary: Expanding the open model universe, NVIDIA today released new open models, data and tools to advance AI across every industry. These models — spanning the NVIDIA Nemotron family for agentic AI, the NVIDIA Cosmos platform for physical AI, the new NVIDIA Alpamayo family for autonomous vehicle development, NVIDIA Isaac GR00T for robotics and NVIDIA Clara […]

items.title: Backdoor Attacks on Prompt-Driven Video Segmentation Foundation Models
items.summary: Prompt-driven Video Segmentation Foundation Models (VSFMs), such as SAM2, are increasingly used in applications including autonomous driving and digital pathology, yet their security risks remain underexplored. We study backdoor attacks against VSFMs and show that directly applying classic attacks such as BadNet is largely ineffective, yielding attack success rates (ASR) below 5%.

items.title: ImagineNav++: Prompting Vision-Language Models as Embodied Navigator through Scene Imagination
items.summary: Visual navigation is a fundamental capability for autonomous home-assistance robots, enabling long-horizon tasks such as object search.

items.title: Pickle Robot adds Tesla veteran as first CFO
items.summary: Jeff Evanson recently joined Pickle Robot as the company's first CFO as the startup has allegedly expanded its partnership with UPS.

items.title: Into the Omniverse: OpenUSD and NVIDIA Halos Accelerate Safety for Robotaxis, Physical AI Systems
items.summary: Physical AI is moving from research labs into the real world, powering intelligent robots and autonomous vehicles (AVs) — such as robotaxis — that must reliably sense, reason and act amid unpredictable conditions.

items.title: OmniDrive-R1: Reinforcement-driven Interleaved Multi-modal Chain-of-Thought for Trustworthy Vision-Language Autonomous Driving
items.summary: The deployment of Vision-Language Models (VLMs) in safety-critical domains like autonomous driving (AD) is critically hindered by reliability failures, most notably object hallucination. This failure stems from their reliance on ungrounded, text-based Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.

items.title: Cheers to AI: ADAM Robot Bartender Makes Drinks at Vegas Golden Knights Game
items.summary: In Las Vegas’s T-Mobile Arena, fans of the Golden Knights are getting more than just hockey — they’re getting a taste of the future. ADAM, a robot developed with NVIDIA Isaac libraries, is pouring drinks and turning heads in one of the NHL’s most exciting venues. ADAM, short for Automated Dual Arm Mixologist, was developed […]

items.title: 1X struck a deal to send its ‘home’ humanoids to factories and warehouses
items.summary: Despite launching as a humanoid robot designed to help consumers around the house, 1X's Neo robots are heading to industrial use cases.

items.title: Robots’ Holiday Wishes Come True: NVIDIA Jetson Platform Offers High-Performance Edge AI at Festive Prices
items.summary: Editor’s note: This blog has been updated to showcase additional recent innovations tapping into the NVIDIA Jetson platform. Developers, researchers, hobbyists and students can take a byte out of holiday shopping this season as NVIDIA has unwrapped special discounts on the NVIDIA Jetson family of developer kits for edge AI and robotics — available through […]

items.title: This Khosla-backed startup can track drones, trucks, and robotaxis, inch by inch
items.summary: Point One Navigation, now valued at $230 million, is building out well beyond automotive.

items.title: Why a researcher is building robots that look and act like bats
items.summary: These palm-sized robots use ultrasound signals to navigate harsh conditions in search and rescue missions.

items.title: AI researchers ’embodied’ an LLM into a robot – and it started channeling Robin Williams
items.summary: AI researchers at Andon Labs embedded various LLMs in a vacuum robot to test how ready they were to be embodied. And hilarity ensued.

items.title: Coco Robotics taps UCLA professor to lead new physical AI research lab
items.summary: Coco Robotics is working toward automating its fleet of delivery robots using its millions of miles of collected data.

items.title: The world is just not quite ready for humanoids yet
items.summary: Despite the amount of money being injected into the industry, humanoids won't be able to learn dexterity -- or the fine motor movements with hands -- rendering them essentially useless.

items.title: SoftBank bulks up its robotics portfolio with ABB Group’s robotics unit
items.summary: SoftBank says this acquisition will help it dive deeper into robotics as it considers physical AI to be the next frontier.

items.title: Former OpenAI and DeepMind researchers raise whopping $300M seed to automate science
items.summary: Periodic Labs has raised from a tech industry who's who, including Andreessen Horowitz, Nvidia, Elad Gil, Jeff Dean, Eric Schmidt, and Jeff Bezos.

items.title: Live demo fails, AI safety wins, and the golden age of robotics
items.summary: This week on Equity, Anthony Ha, Kirsten Korosec, and Max Zeff unpack the biggest moves in AI, robotics, and regulation. Listen to the full episode to hear about: Equity will be back next week. Subscribe wherever you get your podcasts! Equity is TechCrunch’s flagship podcast, produced by Theresa Loconsolo, and posts every Wednesday and Friday. […]

items.title: Why Runway is eyeing the robotics industry for future revenue growth
items.summary: Runway is building up a robotics-focused team and fine-tuning its existing models for robotics and self-driving car customers.

items.title: Tesla is reportedly behind on its pledge to build 5,000 Optimus bots this year
items.summary: Tesla is behind on its goal to produce at least 5,000 Optimus humanoid robots by the end of 2025.

items.title: How TRIC Robotics is reducing pesticide use on strawberries using UV light
items.summary: TRIC Robotics just raised a $5.5 million seed round for its tractor-sized robots that help eliminate bacteria and pests on strawberry plants.

items.title: Diligent Robotics hires two notable Cruise alumni to its leadership team
items.summary: Diligent Robotics hired Cruise's former chief operating officer and former head of AI and robotics into c-suite positions.

items.title: Accelerating Policy Synthesis in Large-Scale MDPs via Hierarchical Adaptive Refinement
items.summary: Software-intensive systems, such as software product lines and robotics, utilise Markov decision processes (MDPs) to capture uncertainty and analyse sequential decision-making problems. Despite the usefulness of conventional policy synthesis methods, they fail to scale to large state spaces.

items.title: K2MUSE: A human lower-limb multimodal walking dataset spanning task and acquisition variability for rehabilitation robotics
items.summary: The natural interaction and control performance of lower limb rehabilitation robots are closely linked to biomechanical information from various human locomotion activities.

items.title: From Action Labels to Sets: Rethinking Action Supervision for Imitation Learning from Corrective Feedback
items.summary: Behavior cloning (BC) optimizes policies by treating human demonstrations as pointwise action labels. While effective with accurate action labels, this formulation is brittle in practice: when human-provided actions are imperfect, treating each label as an exact target can steer the policy away from the underlying desired behavior, particularly when expressive models are used (e.g., energy-based models).

items.title: IKSPARK: Obstacle-Aware Inverse Kinematics via Convex Optimization
items.summary: Inverse kinematics (IK) is central to robot control and motion planning, yet its nonlinear kinematic mapping makes it inherently nonconvex and particularly challenging under complex constraints.

items.title: Adaptive Nonlinear MPC for Trajectory Tracking of An Overactuated Tiltrotor Hexacopter
items.summary: Omnidirectional micro aerial vehicles (OMAVs) are more capable of doing environmentally interactive tasks due to their ability to exert full wrenches while maintaining stable poses. However, OMAVs often incorporate additional actuators and complex mechanical structures to achieve omnidirectionality.
